data.title: AAL, DAL, UAL: Airline Stocks Crash as Jet Fuel Costs Soar
data.description:
The stocks of leading airlines have crashed nearly 10% in the past week as the cost of jet fuel soars following the U.S. attack on Iran.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shares of American Airlines (AAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), and United Airlines (UAL) are each down about 4% on March 6 and headed for a weekly loss of close to 10% as they struggle with rising jet fuel costs as the price of crude oil spikes above $90 a barrel.

Since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28, the price of jet fuel has increased 3.6% to a national average of $6.39 per gallon. Jet fuel prices are expected to move even higher if the conflict in the Middle East is prolonged.

Airline Executives Manage Expectations

At the same time, U.S. airlines are dealing with the fact that thousands of flights in the Middle East and around the world have been canceled since the latest fighting broke out. By some estimates, more than 20,000 flights have been cancelled since Feb. 28.

Airline executives are taking to the airwaves and social media to manage shareholder expectations moving forward. United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby said on March 6 that the spike in jet fuel prices will have a "meaningful" impact on the carrier's first-quarter results, and warned that it could also lead to higher airfares for travelers.

Comparing Airline Stocks

Below is a chart comparing the stocks of American, Delta and United Airlines.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 3 Defense ETFs amid Fears of a Costly Drone War
data.description:
Iran's increasing use of large drone swarms in attacks across the Gulf is raising fears that the region could face a long and costly drone war. According to figures released by Gulf defense ministries and reported by Reuters, Iran had launched more than 540 missiles and over 1,450 drone strikes against regional countries as of March 3. Importantly, drones made up about three-quarters of all the attacks. In many ways, these tactics are similar to what has been seen in Ukraine, where large waves of cheap drones are launched alongside missiles to overwhelm air-defense systems.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, analysts say that Iran's strategy in the Gulf appears designed to stretch U.S. defense systems across multiple countries at the same time. And while Gulf countries have managed to intercept many of the incoming threats, doing so is extremely expensive. In fact, to stop these attacks, countries in the region rely on advanced systems such as the U.S.-made Patriot missile defense system, THAAD interceptors, and fighter jets like Rafales, F-15s, and Eurofighters. However, experts warn that this approach may not be sustainable if the conflict continues for a long period.

This is because some estimates suggest that for every dollar Iran spends producing drones, Gulf states may spend between $20 and $28 trying to destroy them, while a single interceptor missile can cost more than $1 million. Therefore, analysts argue that countries urgently need cheaper counter-drone tools, such as electronic jammers, directed-energy weapons, and automated guns. Meanwhile, the increase in military spending is also attracting attention from investors, especially those looking at defense and aerospace companies. As a result, the following defense ETFs could benefit if the war drags on:

  • ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX)
  • iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA)
  • SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR)

Which Defense ETF Will Benefit the Most?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three ETFs mentioned above, analysts think that ARKX has the most room to run. In fact, ARKX's price target of $40.47 per share implies 28.5% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Trump Seeks 'Unconditional Surrender' from Iran with No Interest in a Deal
data.description:
President Trump urged Iran to surrender on Friday and expressed a lack of interest in a nuclear deal on the seventh day of the conflict.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"There will be no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER!" Trump said in a Truth Social post on Friday. Earlier this week, he told Axios that he must be involved in the selection of the country's next leader, "like with Delcy [Rodriguez] in Venezuela."

Khamenei Succession Looms as Oil Prices Jump

Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is expected to replace his father as Iran's supreme leader, although no official announcement has been made yet. Iran has delayed the announcement due to security concerns.

Tensions in the Middle East have nearly erased all ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, which handles roughly 20% of global oil flows. In the event of a prolonged conflict, higher energy prices could lead to growing inflation and falling consumer demand. The price for a gallon of gas in the U.S. has surged 33.8 cents over the past week, according to AAA.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: BlackRock (BLK) Limits Withdrawals from Private Credit Fund, Sending Shockwaves Across Wall Street
data.description:
Asset manager BlackRock (BLK) is limiting withdrawals from one of its leading private credit funds, sending shockwaves across Wall Street and striking fear into the hearts of investors.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BlackRock is the world's largest asset manager with $12.5 trillion under management. The New York-based firm has announced that it is limiting withdrawals from its $26 billion HPS Corporate Lending Fund, one of its marquee private credit vehicles.

The move by BlackRock is rattling the nerves of investors and other Wall Street firms that were already concerned that trouble is brewing in the opaque private credit industry. BLK stock is down 5% on the news that it has limited withdrawals from the fund.

Managing Shareholder Redemptions

BlackRock says that it has no choice but to limit redemptions from the HPS fund after shareholders requested redemptions of 9.3% of the available shares. BlackRock has capped the repurchase rate at 5%, which totals about $1.2 billion based on market data.

BlackRock said limiting redemptions is the prudent thing to do as it aligns with existing liquidity management for the fund. Without the limit, there would be a structural mismatch between investor capital and the expected duration of private credit loans, leading to greater problems.

While BlackRock's move might be practical, it is rattling an already jittery Wall Street. Private credit funds are facing increased redemption requests amid growing concerns about the industry's lending practices and overall health.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: IBM Uses Quantum-Supercomputer Combo to Model Newly Discovered Half-Möbius Molecule
data.description:
Scientists from IBM (IBM) Research and several universities have created a completely new type of molecule that has never been observed before. The discovery, published in the journal Science, involves what researchers describe as a "half-Möbius" molecule. For context, a Möbius strip is a famous geometric shape that twists once and has only one continuous side. Interestingly, the electrons in this molecule move along a similar twisting path, which gives the structure unusual properties that scientists believe could eventually help develop new materials or advanced electronic technologies.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

To build the molecule, researchers used extremely precise tools that allow them to position atoms one at a time. The resulting molecule contains 13 carbon atoms and two chlorine atoms arranged in a ring. However, unlike typical molecular rings, the electrons traveling around this structure follow a twisting path that rotates about 90 degrees during each loop. Because of this, electrons must travel four full circuits around the ring before returning to their original orientation, which is what gives the molecule its unique half-Möbius behavior.

Importantly, though, understanding how this unusual molecule works required more than standard computer simulations. As a result, the researchers combined quantum computing with traditional supercomputers to model how the electrons behave inside the structure. These simulations confirmed that the molecule can exist in different configurations, such as left-twisted, right-twisted, or untwisted forms. Ultimately, the discovery shows that scientists may eventually be able to design entirely new types of molecules on purpose, while also demonstrating how quantum computers can help solve scientific problems that are extremely difficult for traditional computers to analyze.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 12 Buys, six Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $334.18 per share implies 30.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: S&P 500 Sinks as Unemployment Rate Returns to 4.4%
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) opened Friday down by over 1% after the unemployment rate bounced back to 4.4% in February, one of the highest levels since 2021. The rate was 4.4% in December before edging lower to 4.3% in January.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Furthermore, the U.S. economy shed 92,000 jobs in February, while January and December's job additions were revised lower by a combined 69,000. Since May 2025, the economy has lost 19,000 jobs on a cumulative basis, evidencing the growing weakness in the labor market.

When Will the Fed Cut Rates?

A slowing labor market could influence the Fed to cut rates sooner. Lower rates stimulate the labor market by reducing borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, encouraging companies to invest and hire more workers.

"Significant weakening in the labor market would support a rate cut, but given the risk that higher-for-longer oil prices could trigger another inflation surge, the Fed may feel compelled to remain on the sidelines," said Morgan Stanley Wealth Management Chief Economic Strategist Ellen Zentner. While the central bank is still expected to hold rates steady this month, the odds of a 25-bps rate cut at the April 29 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting rose to 17.7% from 11.4% a day ago.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 3/6/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are down by over 1% on Friday following a lackluster jobs report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In February, the unemployment rate reverted to 4.4% as the U.S. economy shed 92,000 jobs, well below the consensus estimate for 55,000 additions. On top of that, the BLS revised January and December's job additions lower by a combined 69,000. Since May 2025, the economy hasn't added a single job on a net basis.

Higher Unemployment Pressures Consumer Spending

With fewer Americans employed, spending could take a hit, lowering gross domestic product (GDP) in the process. Even the healthcare sector, which has remained resilient in recent months, showed a decline of 28,000 jobs due to a Kaiser Permanente strike.

Lower employment could already be taking a toll on U.S. retail sales, which dropped by 0.2% month-over-month, slightly better than the estimate for a 0.3% decline. Core retail sales, which exclude several volatile items like autos and gasoline, were unchanged and fell short of expectations for a 0.1% rise.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Stock Futures Plunge as Oil Prices Spike amid U.S.-Iran Conflict
data.description:
Stock futures fell on Friday morning as oil prices surged amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.76%, 0.56%, and 0.54%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on March 6.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, benchmark Brent crude futures rose 3.6% to trade above $88 per barrel. Meanwhile, West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures soared 6%, trading at $85.81 per barrel. The U.S.-Iran war has entered its seventh day and continues to disrupt global fuel supplies. 

Qatar's energy minister told The Financial Times that Gulf energy producers may have to shut down production in a move that could push oil to $150 a barrel. He cautioned that the U.S.-Iran conflict could "bring down the economies of the world."

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages ended in the red due to geopolitical concerns. The S&P 500, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite, and the Dow Jones fell 0.56%, 1.61%, and 0.26%, respectively. Overall, the Dow Jones is heading towards its second consecutive week of losses.

Turning to trending stocks, Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares rallied 11% in pre-market trading after the semiconductor company ai-demand">reported solid results and outlook, driven by AI-led demand. In contrast, Gap (GAP) stock was down more 9% after the apparel company missed Q4 earnings expectations.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Bank of America (BAC) Is Telling Investors to Sell Oil Stocks as Crude Prices Soar. Here's Why
data.description:
You might think that now would be an opportune time to buy oil stocks as crude prices soar to their highest level in years. But not according to Bank of America (BAC).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Michael Hartnett, Bank of America's chief investment strategist, says investors should sell oil stocks if the price of crude surpasses $90 a barrel, a level it breached in early trading on March 6. The call might seem counterintuitive, but Hartnett lays out a persuasive argument.

In his weekly research note, Hartnett explains why he thinks there will be a de-escalation of the Iranian war: politics. With gasoline prices up 15% in the last week, Trump's approval ratings on the economy at 40%, and the Midterm U.S. elections on the horizon, the White House will not want a prolonged war and even higher oil prices.

As such, Hartnett is warning that $90 a barrel crude might be as high as oil prices get, and investors should sell at the top.

If the war is mercifully short and risk appetite troughs in March, as Hartnett and team expect, then the bid for what he refers to as the inflation boom beneficiaries should reassert itself. These feature the commodities complex, international stocks
ACWX

-1.07%

and emerging markets
EEM

-0.56%

.

Hartnett warns, however, that without investors adopting bearish positioning and without central banking policy panic, then any recovery in markets might be hard work. Hartnett also reckons a resurgence in Trump's approval ratings is necessary for meaningful improvement in risk assets.

There are other conditions highlighted that need to be in situ at this point for a stock market rally to develop.

It requires the oversold assets like software stocks
IGV

-0.41%

, the Mag7
MAGS

-1.30%

, bitcoin and private credit to bottom and for the overbought, such as gold
GC00

+1.45%

, metals, semiconductors
SOX

-1.02%

, emerging markets and European equities to correct. This positioning realignment would facilitate a better starting point for a rally but for the light to flash green, then the current bidding up of oil and the dollar need to subside.

The warning is clear, though: Hartnett's recommendations are dependent on the conflict being contained and short-lived. If Iran escalates and the war broadens out across the region forcing oil above $100, then an oil price shock is coming.

One aspect of the dollar index being driven back up toward 100 is it persuades Hartnett that rate cuts are less likely. In the last few weeks the U.S. Treasury yield curve has flattened and is starting to price out any Fed easing in the remainder of this year.

Hartnett also suggests that one other signal indicating to traders that the market has flushed out excess bull positioning would be a drop in the S&P 500
SPX

-1.20%

index below 6,600.

One feature of markets at present that Hartnett does find encouraging is the signs that the capital expenditure associated with the development of AI architecture might have peaked, evidenced by Nvidia
NVDA

-0.79%

telling investors its putative $100 billion investment in OpenAI is "no longer on the cards." Any deceleration in that exponential capex growth will afford credit markets relief and may alleviate some of the risks around a "credit event," he said.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: United Airlines (UAL) Stock Slides as the Airline Takes a Firm Stance on Headphones
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) stock fell on Friday after the air travel company said it was done with the noise. The company has updated its official policy to expand the number of things that can get passengers kicked off its flights. The latest entry on this list is failing to use headphones while listening to audio or video content.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Passenger complaints about loud noise during flights have increased, and United Airlines agrees that it has gone too far. What was once proper etiquette has been ignored by some passengers who have taken to playing music through Bluetooth speakers, making flights uncomfortable for others. To avoid further issues, United Airlines has taken this firm stance on headphone use. Carnival (CCL) took a similar step to cut down on passenger complaints when the cruise line banned Bluetooth speakers from its ships last year.

As the policy is new, there will likely be some issues as passengers who prefer not to use headphones adjust to the change. However, those who don't have headphones, or who forgot them, won't have to worry. United Airlines has announced that it will offer a free pair of headphones to passengers who don't have their own in an effort to alleviate future conflicts or complaints.

United Airlines Stock Movement

United Airlines stock was down 4.62% on Friday, extending a 14.66% year-to-date drop. Even so, the shares were still up 15.14% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White
